circular.title: Master Circular - Credit facilities to Scheduled Castes (SCs) & Scheduled Tribes (STs)
circular.summary: This Master Circular consolidates existing guidelines for banks to increase credit flow to Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes. It mandates that banks integrate SC/ST credit planning into the Lead Bank Scheme and Block-level plans, ensuring loan proposals are handled with sympathy. Banks are prohibited from insisting on deposits for government-sponsored poverty alleviation schemes and must ensure rejections are handled by a higher administrative level than the branch.

clauses.text: The district credit plans formulated by the Lead Banks should clearly indicate the linkage of credit with employment and development schemes.
clauses.summary: District credit plans should link credit with employment and development schemes

clauses.text: Banks will have to establish closer liaison with the District Industries Centres for promoting self-employment.
clauses.summary: Banks should establish closer liaison with District Industries Centres

clauses.text: At the block level, a certain weightage is to be given to SCs/STs in the planning process. Credit planning should be weighted in their favour and special bankable schemes suited to them should be drawn up.
clauses.summary: Block-level planning should give weightage to SCs/STs

clauses.text: Banks should periodically review their lending procedures and policies to see that loans are sanctioned in time, are adequate and production-oriented.
clauses.summary: Banks should review lending procedures and policies

clauses.text: While formulating the Block/ District Credit Plan, special focus may be given to villages with sizeable population of SC/ST communities/ specific localities (bastis).
clauses.summary: Block/District Credit Plan should focus on villages with SC/ST communities

clauses.text: Bank staff may help the borrowers in filling up the forms and completing other formalities to ensure credit is received within a stipulated period.
clauses.summary: Bank staff should help borrowers with forms and formalities

clauses.text: Awareness must be created through brochures, visits by field staff, etc. Banks should advise their branches to organize meetings more frequently exclusively for SC/ST beneficiaries.
clauses.summary: Banks should create awareness and organize meetings for SC/ST beneficiaries

clauses.text: Circulars issued by RBI/NABARD should be circulated among the staff for compliance.
clauses.summary: Banks should circulate RBI/NABARD circulars among staff

clauses.text: Banks should not insist on deposits while considering loan applications under Government sponsored poverty alleviation schemes/self-employment programmes from SC/ST borrowers.
clauses.summary: Banks should not insist on deposits for SC/ST borrowers

clauses.text: It should also be ensured that applicable subsidy is not held back while releasing the loan component till the full repayment of bank dues.
clauses.summary: Banks should not hold back subsidies for SC/ST borrowers

clauses.text: Banks should advise their branches/controlling offices to render institutional support to the National Scheduled Tribes Finance & Development Corporation and National Scheduled Castes Finance & Development Corporation.
clauses.summary: Banks should provide institutional support to SC/ST finance corporations

clauses.text: Rejection of SC/STs’ loan applications under government programmes should be done at the next higher level instead of at the branch level and reasons of rejection should be clearly indicated.
clauses.summary: Rejection of SC/ST loan applications should be done at higher level

clauses.text: DAY-NRLM would ensure adequate coverage such that 50% of beneficiaries are SCs/STs.
clauses.summary: DAY-NRLM should ensure 50% beneficiaries are SCs/STs

clauses.text: Banks have been advised to grant eligible borrowers belonging to SCs/STs advances to the extent of not less than 2/5th (40 percent) of total DRI advances.
clauses.summary: Banks should grant 40% of DRI advances to SCs/STs

clauses.text: A special cell should be set up at the Head Office of banks for monitoring the flow of credit to SC/ST beneficiaries.
clauses.summary: Banks should set up special cell for monitoring SC/ST credit

clauses.text: Any major gap or variation in credit flow to SCs/STs on a year to year basis should be reported to the Board as part of the review on the theme of “Financial Inclusion”.
clauses.summary: Banks should report gaps in SC/ST credit flow to Board

clauses.text: Banks should review the measures taken to enhance the flow of credit to SC/ST borrowers on a quarterly basis.
clauses.summary: Banks should review SC/ST credit measures quarterly

obligations.text: District credit plans should link credit with employment and development schemes
obligations.plain_english: Banks should ensure district credit plans consider employment and development schemes for SCs/STs

obligations.text: Banks should establish closer liaison with District Industries Centres
obligations.plain_english: Banks should work closely with District Industries Centres to promote self-employment for SCs/STs

obligations.text: Block-level planning should give weightage to SCs/STs
obligations.plain_english: Banks should give priority to SCs/STs in block-level credit planning

obligations.text: Banks should review lending procedures and policies
obligations.plain_english: Banks should regularly review their lending procedures to ensure they are adequate and production-oriented for SCs/STs

obligations.text: Block/District Credit Plan should focus on villages with SC/ST communities
obligations.plain_english: Banks should focus on villages with significant SC/ST populations when creating Block/District Credit Plans

obligations.text: Bank staff should help borrowers with forms and formalities
obligations.plain_english: Bank staff should assist SC/ST borrowers with completing forms and other formalities

obligations.text: Banks should create awareness and organize meetings for SC/ST beneficiaries
obligations.plain_english: Banks should create awareness about their services and organize meetings specifically for SC/ST beneficiaries

obligations.text: Banks should circulate RBI/NABARD circulars among staff
obligations.plain_english: Banks should ensure that their staff is aware of and complies with RBI and NABARD circulars

obligations.text: Banks should not hold back subsidies for SC/ST borrowers
obligations.plain_english: Banks should ensure that subsidies for SC/ST borrowers are not delayed or withheld

obligations.text: Banks should provide institutional support to SC/ST finance corporations
obligations.plain_english: Banks should support and work with SC/ST finance corporations to promote financial inclusion

obligations.text: Rejection of SC/ST loan applications should be done at higher level
obligations.plain_english: Banks should ensure that rejections of SC/ST loan applications are done at a higher level than the branch level and with clear reasons

obligations.text: DAY-NRLM should ensure 50% beneficiaries are SCs/STs
obligations.plain_english: Banks should ensure that at least 50% of DAY-NRLM beneficiaries are from SC/ST communities

obligations.text: Banks should grant 40% of DRI advances to SCs/STs
obligations.plain_english: Banks should allocate at least 40% of DRI advances to eligible SC/ST borrowers

obligations.text: Banks should set up special cell for monitoring SC/ST credit
obligations.plain_english: Banks should establish a special cell at their Head Office to monitor and report on credit flow to SC/ST beneficiaries

obligations.text: Banks should report gaps in SC/ST credit flow to Board
obligations.plain_english: Banks should report any significant gaps or variations in credit flow to SC/ST beneficiaries to their Board as part of the Financial Inclusion review

obligations.text: Banks should review SC/ST credit measures quarterly
obligations.plain_english: Banks should regularly review the measures taken to enhance credit flow to SC/ST borrowers on a quarterly basis

prohibitions.text: Banks should not insist on deposits for SC/ST borrowers
prohibitions.plain_english: Banks should not require deposits from SC/ST borrowers for government-sponsored poverty alleviation or self-employment schemes

definitions.definition: Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes as recognized under the Constitution of India
definitions.plain_english: Communities recognized by the Indian Constitution as Scheduled Castes or Scheduled Tribes

definitions.definition: A scheme for coordinating activities of banks and other developmental agencies to deliver a coordinated approach to credit in rural areas
definitions.plain_english: A scheme to coordinate bank and developmental agency activities for rural credit

definitions.definition: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission
definitions.plain_english: A government program aimed at rural livelihoods and poverty alleviation

definitions.definition: Differential Rate of Interest Scheme providing concessional credit at 4% p.a.
definitions.plain_english: A scheme offering loans at a reduced interest rate of 4% per annum

definitions.definition: Credit Enhancement Guarantee Scheme for Scheduled Castes
definitions.plain_english: A guarantee scheme to enhance credit access for Scheduled Caste entrepreneurs

definitions.definition: State Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes Finance and Development Corporation
definitions.plain_english: State-level corporations for financing and developing Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe communities

references.circular_number: Master Direction on Priority Sector Lending
